original: WHEREAS:
ollama_generated:
WHEREAS:

In consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties hereto agree as follows:

(a) Obligations of Party A: Instead of:
Party A shall have a continuing obligation to perform its duties in a timely and diligent manner.

Party A will make reasonable efforts to fulfill their obligations in a timely fashion, but shall not be held liable for delays beyond their control.

(b) Condition for Party B's Payment: Instead of:
In the event that Party A fails to perform its duties as set forth herein, then Party B shall have no obligation to make any payments to Party A.

Payment by Party B to Party A will be contingent upon the latter's successful and timely completion of their obligations, but is subject to deductions for any reasonable costs incurred due to delays or failures on Party A's part.

(c) Responsibility Shifted to Party A: Instead of:
In the event of a dispute arising out of this Agreement, the parties shall endeavor to resolve the matter through good faith negotiations. If such negotiations fail, then either party may submit the dispute to binding arbitration.

Any disputes arising out of this Agreement shall be resolved solely by Party A, who shall have the final decision-making authority in all matters related to the interpretation and enforcement of this Agreement. Party B agrees to comply with any decisions made by Party A without further recourse or contestation.

modified: "Control" or "ownership" shall be established through the direct or indirect possession of less than fifty (49) percent of the nominal value of the issued equity share capital, or of less than fifty (49) percent of the shares carrying voting rights for the election of directors or individuals undertaking comparable responsibilities. Clause 2.9:
Parties acknowledge that their activities fall under the purview of both Dutch and EU regulations, including but not limited to EU-directives, the Dutch Telecommunications Act, Dutch Competition Act, and the Dutch Data Protection Act. While acknowledging these obligations, Parties will strive for compliance with all applicable regulations; however, any failure to fully comply shall not automatically result in breach of contract, provided that such non-compliance is immediately reported to the relevant authorities, including OPTA, as required by article 2.1 of the Dutch Telecommunications Act. original: 22. DISPUTE  RESOLUTION Any dispute, excluding invoice disputes as described in Appendix 7,  controversy or claim arising out of or in connection with any aspect of this Agreement, or the breach, termination or invalidity thereof, shall:
ollama_generated:
22. DISPUTE RESOLUTION Any contention, excluding invoice disputes detailed in Appendix 7, that may arise concerning this Agreement, its interpretation, a violation, termination, or nullification, will be resolved through the following mechanisms:

1. Negotiation: Parties shall first attempt to resolve any dispute through good faith negotiations for a period of thirty (30) days. If the parties fail to reach an agreement during this time, they may proceed to step 2.

2. Mediation: The parties will then engage in mediation conducted by a mutually agreed-upon mediator. If the dispute is not resolved within sixty (60) days of commencing mediation, either party may initiate arbitration as provided in section 23.

3. Arbitration: In the event that mediation is unsuccessful, the dispute shall be finally settled by binding arbitration conducted in accordance with the Rules of the American Arbitration Association (AAA) then in effect. Judgment upon any award rendered may be entered in any court having jurisdiction.

This modification aims to provide a more structured and cooperative approach to dispute resolution, allowing for negotiations and mediation before proceeding to arbitration. It also introduces a time frame for each step of the process to ensure a timely resolution.
